description: Fix two data-loss bugs in the browser extension's network capture module where getRequests(clear=true) and getWsFrames(clear=true) incorrectly clear internal tracking maps needed for ongoing capture, silently dropping in-flight HTTP requests and all subsequent WebSocket frames from existing connections.

userStories.title: Fix getWsFrames clear to preserve active WebSocket connection tracking
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.getWebSocketFrames with clear=true, I want the buffer to be cleared without losing track of active WebSocket connections so that subsequent frames from existing connections are still captured.
acceptanceCriteria: getWsFrames(tabId, true) clears the wsFrames array but does NOT clear wsFramesByRequestId
After a clear-read, new WebSocket frames from existing connections are still captured in the wsFrames buffer
stopCapture still clears wsFramesByRequestId as part of full teardown
Network.webSocketClosed still deletes individual entries from wsFramesByRequestId

userStories.title: Fix getRequests clear to preserve in-flight request tracking
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.getNetworkRequests with clear=true, I want the buffer to be cleared without losing in-flight HTTP requests so that requests currently being processed still appear in subsequent reads.
acceptanceCriteria: getRequests(tabId, true) clears the requests array but does NOT clear pendingRequests or requestIdToRequest
After a clear-read, in-flight HTTP requests (sent but not yet responded) are still tracked and appear in subsequent getRequests calls
After a clear-read, response bodies for recently-completed requests are still attached by the loadingFinished handler
stopCapture still fully clears all maps as part of full teardown

userStories.title: Add unit tests for network capture buffer clear behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests that verify getRequests(clear=true) and getWsFrames(clear=true) preserve internal tracking state so that these bugs don't regress.
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies that after getWsFrames(tabId, true), new WebSocket frames from existing connections are still captured
Test verifies that after getRequests(tabId, true), in-flight HTTP requests still appear in subsequent reads
Test verifies that after getRequests(tabId, true), response bodies are still attached for recently-completed requests
Test verifies that stopCapture fully clears all internal state including tracking maps
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.test.ts (existing test file — add new tests)

The existing test file already mocks chrome.debugger APIs. Follow the existing test patterns. Key test scenarios:

1. **WS frames survive clear**: Start capture → simulate webSocketCreated (adds to wsFramesByRequestId) → simulate webSocketFrameReceived (adds frame) → getWsFrames(tabId, true) to read+clear → simulate another webSocketFrameReceived for the same requestId → getWsFrames(tabId, false) should return the new frame.

2. **In-flight requests survive clear**: Start capture → simulate requestWillBeSent (adds to pendingRequests) → getRequests(tabId, true) to read+clear → simulate responseReceived for the same requestId → getRequests(tabId, false) should return the completed request.

3. **Response body attachment survives clear**: Start capture → simulate requestWillBeSent → simulate responseReceived (moves to requestIdToRequest) → getRequests(tabId, true) to read+clear → simulate loadingFinished for the same requestId → the request's responseBody should be populated.

4. **stopCapture clears everything**: Start capture → add some state → stopCapture → verify captures map no longer has the tabId entry.

The existing tests use a pattern of manually calling the chrome.debugger.onEvent listener with simulated CDP events. Look at the existing test file to follow this pattern.

Important: the test file path is platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.test.ts — read it first to understand the existing test structure and mocking patterns before adding new tests.
